xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers package¶

GemNet layer modules: basis functions, interaction blocks, embeddings, and utilities.

Submodules¶

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.atom_update module¶

Atom and output update blocks aggregating edge messages into atom embeddings.

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.atom_update.AtomUpdateBlock(emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_rbf, nHidden, activation, scale_file, name)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Aggregate edge message embeddings into updated atom embeddings.

Parameters:

  • emb_size_atom (int) – Atom embedding dimension (output).

  • emb_size_edge (int) – Edge embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_rbf (int) – Radial basis embedding dimension.

  • nHidden (int) – Number of residual layers in the MLP.

  • activation (str) – Activation function name.

  • scale_file (str | None) – Path to JSON scale-factor file, or None.

  • name (str) – Block name (used to look up scale factors).

__init__(emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_rbf, nHidden, activation, scale_file, name)[source]¶

Initialize AtomUpdateBlock.

get_mlp(units, nHidden, activation)[source]¶

Build the atom-update MLP: one dense layer followed by residual blocks.

Parameters:

  • units (int) – Feature dimension for the residual layers.

  • nHidden (int) – Number of residual layers.

  • activation (str) – Activation function name.

Return type:

ModuleList

Returns:

ModuleList containing the dense layer and residual blocks.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize all sub-layer weights.

Return type:

None

forward(h, m, rbf, id_j)[source]¶

Aggregate edge messages and update atom embeddings.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Current atom embeddings, shape (nAtoms, emb_size_atom) (used only to determine nAtoms).

  • m (Tensor) – Edge embeddings, shape (nEdges, emb_size_edge).

  • rbf (Tensor) – Projected radial basis features, shape (nEdges, emb_size_rbf).

  • id_j (Tensor) – Target atom index for each edge, shape (nEdges,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Updated atom embeddings of shape (nAtoms, emb_size_atom).

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.atom_update.OutputBlock(emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_rbf, nHidden, num_targets, activation, output_init, scale_file, name)[source]¶

Bases: AtomUpdateBlock

Atom update block followed by a final dense projection to target outputs.

Extends AtomUpdateBlock by adding a linear output layer that projects atom embeddings to the prediction target dimension.

Parameters:

  • emb_size_atom (int) – Atom embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_edge (int) – Edge embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_rbf (int) – Radial basis embedding dimension.

  • nHidden (int) – Number of residual layers in the shared MLP.

  • num_targets (int) – Output dimension per atom.

  • activation (str) – Activation function name.

  • output_init (str) – Initialization strategy for the output weight matrix. Either "HeOrthogonal" or "zeros".

  • scale_file (str | None) – Path to JSON scale-factor file, or None.

  • name (str) – Block name (used to look up scale factors).

__init__(emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_rbf, nHidden, num_targets, activation, output_init, scale_file, name)[source]¶

Initialize OutputBlock.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize all weights, applying the configured output initializer.

Return type:

None

forward(h, m, rbf, id_j)[source]¶

Aggregate edge messages and return per-atom target predictions.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Current atom embeddings, shape (nAtoms, emb_size_atom) (used only to determine nAtoms).

  • m (Tensor) – Edge embeddings, shape (nEdges, emb_size_edge).

  • rbf (Tensor) – Projected radial basis features, shape (nEdges, emb_size_rbf).

  • id_j (Tensor) – Target atom index for each edge, shape (nEdges,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Per-atom predictions of shape (nAtoms, num_targets).

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.base module¶

Base dense and residual layer building blocks for GemNet.

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.base.Dense(in_features, out_features, bias=False, activation=None)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Dense (linear) layer with optional scaled SiLU activation.

Parameters:

  • in_features (int) – Input feature dimension.

  • out_features (int) – Output feature dimension.

  • bias (bool) – If True, include a learnable bias term.

  • activation (str | None) – Activation function name. Supported values are "swish" / "silu" (scaled SiLU) and None (identity).

__init__(in_features, out_features, bias=False, activation=None)[source]¶

Initialize Dense.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize linear weights with He-orthogonal init and zero bias.

Return type:

None

forward(x)[source]¶

Apply the linear transform followed by the activation.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input features of shape (*, in_features).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Output features of shape (*, out_features).

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.base.ScaledSiLU[source]¶

Bases: Module

SiLU (Swish) activation scaled by 1 / 0.6 for variance preservation.

__init__()[source]¶

Initialize ScaledSiLU.

forward(x)[source]¶

Apply scaled SiLU activation.

Parameters:

x (Tensor) – Input tensor.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Activated tensor scaled by 1 / 0.6.

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.base.ResidualLayer(units, activation, nLayers=2)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Residual block with output scaled by 1 / sqrt(2).

Parameters:

  • units (int) – Feature dimension (input = output).

  • activation (str) – Activation function name (forwarded to Dense).

  • nLayers (int) – Number of Dense layers in the residual branch.

__init__(units, activation, nLayers=2)[source]¶

Initialize ResidualLayer.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize all inner Dense layers.

Return type:

None

forward(inputs)[source]¶

Apply the residual transformation.

Parameters:

inputs (Tensor) – Input features of shape (*, units).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Output features of shape (*, units), scaled by 1 / sqrt(2).

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.basis module¶

Bessel, spherical, and tensor basis layers with associated mathematical utilities.

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.basis.BesselBasisLayer(num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent)[source]¶

Bases: Module

1D Bessel radial basis functions with smooth cutoff envelope.

Parameters:

  • num_radial (int) – Number of basis functions (controls maximum frequency).

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff distance in A.

  • envelope_exponent (int) – Exponent p of the polynomial cutoff envelope.

__init__(num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent)[source]¶

Initialize BesselBasisLayer.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize frequencies to their canonical values pi * [1, ..., num_radial].

Return type:

None

forward(d)[source]¶

Evaluate the Bessel basis on a set of edge distances.

Parameters:

d (Tensor) – Edge distances in A, shape (nEdges,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Basis features of shape (nEdges, num_radial).

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.basis.SphericalBasisLayer(num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, efficient=False)[source]¶

Bases: Module

2D Fourier-Bessel basis (radial x spherical harmonic, single angle).

Parameters:

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical harmonics (controls angular frequency).

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions per spherical harmonic.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff distance in A.

  • envelope_exponent (int) – Exponent p of the polynomial cutoff envelope.

  • efficient (bool) – If True, use the memory-efficient sparse-dense formulation (returns a tuple); otherwise returns a dense tensor.

__init__(num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, efficient=False)[source]¶

Initialize SphericalBasisLayer.

forward(D_ca, Angle_cab, id3_reduce_ca, Kidx)[source]¶

Evaluate the 2D spherical basis on triplet geometry.

Parameters:

  • D_ca (Tensor) – Edge distances c -> a in A, shape (nEdges,).

  • Angle_cab (Tensor) – Triplet angles at atom a (c-a-b), shape (nTriplets,).

  • id3_reduce_ca (Tensor) – Edge index c -> a for each triplet, shape (nTriplets,).

  • Kidx (Tensor) – Neighbor index within the sparse dense matrix, shape (nTriplets,).

Returns:

dense basis of shape (nTriplets, num_spherical * num_radial).

If efficient=True: tuple (rbf_env, sph2) where rbf_env has shape (num_spherical, nEdges, num_radial) and sph2 has shape (nEdges, Kmax, num_spherical).

Return type:

Tensor | tuple[Tensor, Tensor]

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.basis.TensorBasisLayer(num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, efficient=False)[source]¶

Bases: Module

3D Fourier-Bessel basis (radial x two spherical harmonics, two angles).

Parameters:

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical harmonics (controls angular frequency).

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions per spherical harmonic.

  • cutoff (float) – Radial cutoff distance in A.

  • envelope_exponent (int) – Exponent p of the polynomial cutoff envelope.

  • efficient (bool) – If True, use the memory-efficient sparse-dense formulation (returns a tuple); otherwise returns a dense tensor.

__init__(num_spherical, num_radial, cutoff, envelope_exponent, efficient=False)[source]¶

Initialize TensorBasisLayer.

forward(D_ca, Alpha_cab, Theta_cabd, id4_reduce_ca, Kidx)[source]¶

Evaluate the 3D spherical basis on quadruplet geometry.

Parameters:

  • D_ca (Tensor) – Edge distances c -> a in A, shape (nEdges,).

  • Alpha_cab (Tensor) – First angle (c-a-b) for each quadruplet, shape (nQuadruplets,).

  • Theta_cabd (Tensor) – Second angle (dihedral-like c-a-b-d), shape (nQuadruplets,).

  • id4_reduce_ca (Tensor) – Quadruplet reduce index c -> a, shape (nQuadruplets,).

  • Kidx (Tensor) – Neighbor index within the sparse dense matrix, shape (nQuadruplets,).

Returns:

dense basis of shape (nQuadruplets, num_spherical**2 * num_radial).

If efficient=True: tuple (rbf_env, sph2) where rbf_env has shape (num_spherical**2, nEdges, num_radial) and sph2 has shape (nEdges, Kmax, num_spherical**2).

Return type:

Tensor | tuple[Tensor, Tensor]

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.basis.Jn(r, n)[source]¶

Evaluate the spherical Bessel function of order n at r.

Parameters:

  • r (float) – Evaluation point.

  • n (int) – Order of the spherical Bessel function. Non-negative integer.

Return type:

floating[Any]

Returns:

Value of j_n(r).

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.basis.Jn_zeros(n, k)[source]¶

Compute the first k zeros of the spherical Bessel functions up to order n.

Parameters:

  • n (int) – Maximum order (exclusive). Zeros for orders 0, 1, …, n-1 are computed.

  • k (int) – Number of zeros to compute per order.

Return type:

ndarray[tuple[Any, ...], dtype[single]]

Returns:

Array of shape (n, k) containing the zeros.

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.basis.spherical_bessel_formulas(n)[source]¶

Compute sympy formulas for the spherical Bessel functions up to order n.

Parameters:

n (int) – Maximum order (exclusive). Formulas for orders 0, 1, …, n-1 are returned.

Return type:

list[Expr]

Returns:

List of n sympy expressions in x.

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.basis.bessel_basis(n, k)[source]¶

Compute sympy formulas for the normalized, rescaled spherical Bessel basis.

Parameters:

  • n (int) – Maximum order (exclusive).

  • k (int) – Number of basis functions per order (maximum frequency, exclusive).

Return type:

list[list[Expr]]

Returns:

Nested list of shape (n, k) containing sympy expressions in x representing the normalized basis functions.

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.basis.sph_harm_prefactor(l, m)[source]¶

Compute the constant pre-factor for real spherical harmonic Y_l^m.

Pre-factor: sqrt((2l+1) / (4*pi) * (l-|m|)! / (l+|m|)!).

Parameters:

  • l (int) – Degree of the spherical harmonic. Must satisfy l >= 0.

  • m (int) – Order of the spherical harmonic. Must satisfy -l <= m <= l.

Return type:

float

Returns:

Pre-factor value.

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.basis.associated_legendre_polynomials(L, zero_m_only=True, pos_m_only=True)[source]¶

Compute sympy formulas for the associated Legendre polynomials up to degree L.

Parameters:

  • L (int) – Maximum degree (exclusive). Polynomials for degrees 0, 1, …, L-1.

  • zero_m_only (bool) – If True, compute only the m = 0 polynomials.

  • pos_m_only (bool) – If True, compute only the m >= 0 polynomials. Overridden by zero_m_only.

Return type:

list[list[Any]]

Returns:

Nested list where result[l] has length 2*l + 1 and result[l][m] is the sympy formula for P_l^m. Entries are 0 (integer) for uncomputed orders.

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.basis.real_sph_harm(L, spherical_coordinates, zero_m_only=True)[source]¶

Compute sympy formulas for the real spherical harmonics up to degree L.

The variables are either spherical coordinates (phi, theta) or Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z) on the unit sphere.

Parameters:

  • L (int) – Maximum degree (exclusive). Harmonics for degrees 0, 1, …, L-1.

  • spherical_coordinates (bool) – If True, formulas use phi and theta. If False, formulas use x, y, and z.

  • zero_m_only (bool) – If True, compute only the m = 0 harmonics.

Return type:

list[list[Any]]

Returns:

Nested list where result[l] has length 1 if zero_m_only is True, otherwise length 2*l + 1. Each entry is a sympy expression or 0 for uncomputed harmonics.

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.efficient module¶

Memory-efficient down-projection, Hadamard, and bilinear interaction layers for GemNet.

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.efficient.EfficientInteractionDownProjection(num_spherical, num_radial, emb_size_interm)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Down-projection used in the efficient reformulation of the basis interaction.

Projects the radial basis component of the interaction from num_radial dimensions to emb_size_interm using a learned weight tensor.

Parameters:

  • num_spherical (int) – Number of spherical harmonics (controls the first tensor dimension of the weight).

  • num_radial (int) – Number of radial basis functions.

  • emb_size_interm (int) – Intermediate (down-projected) embedding size.

__init__(num_spherical, num_radial, emb_size_interm)[source]¶

Initialize EfficientInteractionDownProjection.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize the weight tensor with He-orthogonal init.

Return type:

None

forward(tbf)[source]¶

Apply the down-projection to the radial basis component.

Parameters:

tbf (tuple[Tensor, Tensor]) –

Tuple (rbf_env, sph) where

  • rbf_env: shape (num_spherical, nEdges, num_radial).

  • sph: shape (nEdges, Kmax, num_spherical).

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor]

Returns:

Tuple (rbf_W1, sph) where

  • rbf_W1: shape (nEdges, emb_size_interm, num_spherical).

  • sph: shape (nEdges, num_spherical, Kmax).

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.efficient.EfficientInteractionHadamard(emb_size_interm, emb_size)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Efficient Hadamard product and summation over neighbor edge embeddings.

Parameters:

  • emb_size_interm (int) – Intermediate (down-projected) embedding size.

  • emb_size (int) – Edge embedding size.

__init__(emb_size_interm, emb_size)[source]¶

Initialize EfficientInteractionHadamard.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize the weight tensor with He-orthogonal init.

Return type:

None

forward(basis, m, id_reduce, Kidx)[source]¶

Compute the Hadamard-based edge message aggregation.

Parameters:

  • basis (tuple[Tensor, Tensor]) –

    Tuple (rbf_W1, sph) from EfficientInteractionDownProjection:

    • rbf_W1: shape (nEdges, emb_size_interm, num_spherical).

    • sph: shape (nEdges, num_spherical, Kmax).

  • m (Tensor) – Neighbor edge embeddings (m_db for quadruplets, m_ba for triplets), shape (nTriplets_or_Quadruplets, emb_size).

  • id_reduce (Tensor) – Index mapping each triplet/quadruplet to its target edge, shape (nTriplets_or_Quadruplets,).

  • Kidx (Tensor) – Neighbor position within the zero-padded dense matrix, shape (nTriplets_or_Quadruplets,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Aggregated edge embeddings of shape (nEdges, emb_size).

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.efficient.EfficientInteractionBilinear(emb_size, emb_size_interm, units_out)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Efficient bilinear layer and subsequent summation over neighbor edge embeddings.

Parameters:

  • emb_size (int) – Edge embedding size.

  • emb_size_interm (int) – Intermediate (down-projected) embedding size.

  • units_out (int) – Output embedding size of the bilinear layer.

__init__(emb_size, emb_size_interm, units_out)[source]¶

Initialize EfficientInteractionBilinear.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize the weight tensor with He-orthogonal init.

Return type:

None

forward(basis, m, id_reduce, Kidx)[source]¶

Compute the bilinear edge message aggregation.

Parameters:

  • basis (tuple[Tensor, Tensor]) –

    Tuple (rbf_W1, sph) from EfficientInteractionDownProjection:

    • rbf_W1: shape (nEdges, emb_size_interm, num_spherical).

    • sph: shape (nEdges, num_spherical, Kmax).

  • m (Tensor) – Neighbor edge embeddings, shape (nTriplets_or_Quadruplets, emb_size).

  • id_reduce (Tensor) – Index mapping each triplet/quadruplet to its target edge, shape (nTriplets_or_Quadruplets,).

  • Kidx (Tensor) – Neighbor position within the zero-padded dense matrix, shape (nTriplets_or_Quadruplets,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Aggregated edge embeddings of shape (nEdges, units_out).

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.embedding module¶

Atom and edge embedding modules for GemNet.

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.embedding.AtomEmbedding(emb_size, num_elements=94)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Initial atom embeddings based on atomic number.

Parameters:

  • emb_size (int) – Atom embedding dimension.

  • num_elements (int) – Number of distinct element types in the embedding table. The default of 94 supports atomic numbers 1-94 (up to Pu). Internally uses z - 1 indexing.

__init__(emb_size, num_elements=94)[source]¶

Initialize AtomEmbedding.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize the embedding table with uniform distribution in [-sqrt(3), sqrt(3)].

Return type:

None

forward(z)[source]¶

Look up atom embeddings by atomic number.

Parameters:

z (Tensor) – Atomic numbers, shape (nAtoms,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Atom embeddings of shape (nAtoms, emb_size).

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.embedding.EdgeEmbedding(atom_features, edge_features, out_features, activation)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Edge embeddings from atom-embedding concatenation followed by a dense layer.

Concatenates the embeddings of the source and target atoms with the edge radial basis features, then applies a Dense projection.

Parameters:

  • atom_features (int) – Atom embedding dimension.

  • edge_features (int) – Edge radial basis feature dimension.

  • out_features (int) – Output edge embedding dimension.

  • activation (str) – Activation function name.

__init__(atom_features, edge_features, out_features, activation)[source]¶

Initialize EdgeEmbedding.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize the inner dense layer.

Return type:

None

forward(h, m_rbf, idnb_a, idnb_c)[source]¶

Compute edge embeddings from atom embeddings and edge features.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Atom embeddings, shape (nAtoms, atom_features).

  • m_rbf (Tensor) – Per-edge features (RBF in the embedding block, m_ca in interaction blocks), shape (nEdges, edge_features).

  • idnb_a (Tensor) – Source atom index for each edge, shape (nEdges,). The historical name is preserved for API compatibility.

  • idnb_c (Tensor) – Target atom index for each edge, shape (nEdges,). The historical name is preserved for API compatibility.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Edge embeddings of shape (nEdges, out_features).

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.envelope module¶

Smooth polynomial cutoff envelope function for GemNet basis layers.

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.envelope.Envelope(p)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Smooth polynomial cutoff envelope that enforces a smooth boundary at d = cutoff.

The function is 1 at d = 0, smoothly decays to 0 at d = cutoff, and is identically 0 for d >= cutoff.

Parameters:

p (int) – Exponent of the envelope polynomial. Must be positive.

__init__(p)[source]¶

Initialize Envelope.

forward(d_scaled)[source]¶

Evaluate the envelope function.

Parameters:

d_scaled (Tensor) – Distances scaled by the cutoff (d / cutoff), shape (*,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Envelope values in [0, 1], shape (*,). Exactly 0 for d_scaled >= 1.

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.interaction module¶

Interaction blocks for GemNet-T (triplets only) and GemNet-Q (quadruplets + triplets).

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.interaction.InteractionBlock(emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_trip, emb_size_quad, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, emb_size_sbf, emb_size_bil_trip, emb_size_bil_quad, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_concat, num_atom, activation, scale_file, name='Interaction')[source]¶

Bases: Module

Full interaction block for GemNet-Q/dQ (quadruplets + triplets).

Parameters:

  • emb_size_atom (int) – Atom embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_edge (int) – Edge embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_trip (int) – Down-projected embedding size in the triplet block.

  • emb_size_quad (int) – Down-projected embedding size in the quadruplet block.

  • emb_size_rbf (int) – Radial basis embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_cbf (int) – Circular basis embedding dimension (one angle).

  • emb_size_sbf (int) – Spherical basis embedding dimension (two angles).

  • emb_size_bil_trip (int) – Edge embedding size after the bilinear layer in the triplet block.

  • emb_size_bil_quad (int) – Edge embedding size after the bilinear layer in the quadruplet block.

  • num_before_skip (int) – Number of residual blocks before the skip connection.

  • num_after_skip (int) – Number of residual blocks after the skip connection.

  • num_concat (int) – Number of residual blocks after the atom-edge concatenation.

  • num_atom (int) – Number of residual blocks in the atom update block.

  • activation (str) – Activation function name.

  • scale_file (str | None) – Path to JSON scale-factor file, or None.

  • name (str) – Block name (used to look up scale factors).

__init__(emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_trip, emb_size_quad, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, emb_size_sbf, emb_size_bil_trip, emb_size_bil_quad, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_concat, num_atom, activation, scale_file, name='Interaction')[source]¶

Initialize InteractionBlock.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize all sub-layer weights.

Return type:

None

forward(h, m, rbf4, cbf4, sbf4, Kidx4, rbf3, cbf3, Kidx3, id_swap, id3_expand_ba, id3_reduce_ca, id4_reduce_ca, id4_expand_intm_db, id4_expand_abd, rbf_h, id_c, id_a)[source]¶

Run one quadruplet + triplet interaction step.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Atom embeddings, shape (nAtoms, emb_size_atom).

  • m (Tensor) – Edge embeddings (c -> a), shape (nEdges, emb_size_edge).

  • rbf4 (Tensor) – Down-projected radial basis for quadruplets, shape (nEdges, emb_size_rbf).

  • cbf4 (Tensor) – Down-projected circular basis for quadruplets, shape (nIntEdges, emb_size_cbf).

  • sbf4 (tuple[Tensor, Tensor]) – Down-projected spherical basis for quadruplets (tuple from EfficientInteractionDownProjection).

  • Kidx4 (Tensor) – Neighbor index for the quadruplet sparse dense matrix.

  • rbf3 (Tensor) – Down-projected radial basis for triplets, shape (nEdges, emb_size_rbf).

  • cbf3 (tuple[Tensor, Tensor]) – Down-projected circular basis for triplets (tuple from EfficientInteractionDownProjection).

  • Kidx3 (Tensor) – Neighbor index for the triplet sparse dense matrix.

  • id_swap (Tensor) – Index mapping each edge to its reverse, shape (nEdges,).

  • id3_expand_ba (Tensor) – Triplet expand index b -> a, shape (nTriplets,).

  • id3_reduce_ca (Tensor) – Triplet reduce index c -> a, shape (nTriplets,).

  • id4_reduce_ca (Tensor) – Quadruplet reduce index c -> a.

  • id4_expand_intm_db (Tensor) – Intermediate-triplet expand index d -> b.

  • id4_expand_abd (Tensor) – Quadruplet expand index for a-b-d.

  • rbf_h (Tensor) – Shared radial basis for the atom update block, shape (nEdges, emb_size_rbf).

  • id_c (Tensor) – Source atom index for each edge, shape (nEdges,).

  • id_a (Tensor) – Target atom index for each edge, shape (nEdges,).

Returns:

  • h: Updated atom embeddings, shape (nAtoms, emb_size_atom).

  • m: Updated edge embeddings, shape (nEdges, emb_size_edge).

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor]

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.interaction.InteractionBlockTripletsOnly(emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_trip, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, emb_size_bil_trip, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_concat, num_atom, activation, scale_file, name='Interaction', **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Interaction block for GemNet-T/dT (triplets only, no quadruplets).

Parameters:

  • emb_size_atom (int) – Atom embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_edge (int) – Edge embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_trip (int) – Down-projected embedding size in the triplet block.

  • emb_size_rbf (int) – Radial basis embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_cbf (int) – Circular basis embedding dimension (one angle).

  • emb_size_bil_trip (int) – Edge embedding size after the bilinear layer in the triplet block.

  • num_before_skip (int) – Number of residual blocks before the skip connection.

  • num_after_skip (int) – Number of residual blocks after the skip connection.

  • num_concat (int) – Number of residual blocks after the atom-edge concatenation.

  • num_atom (int) – Number of residual blocks in the atom update block.

  • activation (str) – Activation function name.

  • scale_file (str | None) – Path to JSON scale-factor file, or None.

  • name (str) – Block name (used to look up scale factors).

  • **kwargs (int) – Extra keyword arguments accepted and ignored for interface compatibility with InteractionBlock.

__init__(emb_size_atom, emb_size_edge, emb_size_trip, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, emb_size_bil_trip, num_before_skip, num_after_skip, num_concat, num_atom, activation, scale_file, name='Interaction', **kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize InteractionBlockTripletsOnly.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize all sub-layer weights.

Return type:

None

forward(h, m, rbf3, cbf3, Kidx3, id_swap, id3_expand_ba, id3_reduce_ca, rbf_h, id_c, id_a, **kwargs)[source]¶

Run one triplet-only interaction step.

Parameters:

  • h (Tensor) – Atom embeddings, shape (nAtoms, emb_size_atom).

  • m (Tensor) – Edge embeddings (c -> a), shape (nEdges, emb_size_edge).

  • rbf3 (Tensor) – Down-projected radial basis for triplets, shape (nEdges, emb_size_rbf).

  • cbf3 (tuple[Tensor, Tensor]) – Down-projected circular basis for triplets (tuple from EfficientInteractionDownProjection).

  • Kidx3 (Tensor) – Neighbor index for the triplet sparse dense matrix.

  • id_swap (Tensor) – Index mapping each edge to its reverse, shape (nEdges,).

  • id3_expand_ba (Tensor) – Triplet expand index b -> a, shape (nTriplets,).

  • id3_reduce_ca (Tensor) – Triplet reduce index c -> a, shape (nTriplets,).

  • rbf_h (Tensor) – Shared radial basis for the atom update block, shape (nEdges, emb_size_rbf).

  • id_c (Tensor) – Source atom index for each edge, shape (nEdges,).

  • id_a (Tensor) – Target atom index for each edge, shape (nEdges,).

  • **kwargs (Tensor | None) – Extra keyword arguments accepted and ignored for interface compatibility with InteractionBlock.

Returns:

  • h: Updated atom embeddings, shape (nAtoms, emb_size_atom).

  • m: Updated edge embeddings, shape (nEdges, emb_size_edge).

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor]

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.interaction.QuadrupletInteraction(emb_size_edge, emb_size_quad, emb_size_bilinear, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, emb_size_sbf, activation, scale_file, name='QuadrupletInteraction')[source]¶

Bases: Module

Quadruplet-based message passing block.

Parameters:

  • emb_size_edge (int) – Edge embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_quad (int) – Down-projected edge embedding size (after Hadamard with RBF).

  • emb_size_bilinear (int) – Edge embedding size after the bilinear layer.

  • emb_size_rbf (int) – Radial basis embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_cbf (int) – Circular basis embedding dimension (one angle).

  • emb_size_sbf (int) – Spherical basis embedding dimension (two angles).

  • activation (str) – Activation function name.

  • scale_file (str | None) – Path to JSON scale-factor file, or None.

  • name (str) – Block name (used to look up scale factors).

__init__(emb_size_edge, emb_size_quad, emb_size_bilinear, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, emb_size_sbf, activation, scale_file, name='QuadrupletInteraction')[source]¶

Initialize QuadrupletInteraction.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize all sub-layer weights.

Return type:

None

forward(m, rbf, cbf, sbf, Kidx4, id_swap, id4_reduce_ca, id4_expand_intm_db, id4_expand_abd)[source]¶

Run the quadruplet interaction and return updated edge embeddings.

Parameters:

  • m (Tensor) – Edge embeddings, shape (nEdges, emb_size_edge).

  • rbf (Tensor) – Projected radial basis, shape (nEdges, emb_size_rbf).

  • cbf (Tensor) – Projected circular basis, shape (nIntEdges, emb_size_cbf).

  • sbf (tuple[Tensor, Tensor]) – Projected spherical basis tuple (from EfficientInteractionDownProjection).

  • Kidx4 (Tensor) – Neighbor index for the quadruplet sparse dense matrix.

  • id_swap (Tensor) – Index mapping each edge to its reverse, shape (nEdges,).

  • id4_reduce_ca (Tensor) – Quadruplet reduce index c -> a.

  • id4_expand_intm_db (Tensor) – Intermediate-triplet expand index d -> b.

  • id4_expand_abd (Tensor) – Quadruplet expand index for a-b-d.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Updated edge embeddings of shape (nEdges, emb_size_edge).

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.interaction.TripletInteraction(emb_size_edge, emb_size_trip, emb_size_bilinear, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, activation, scale_file, name='TripletInteraction')[source]¶

Bases: Module

Triplet-based message passing block.

Parameters:

  • emb_size_edge (int) – Edge embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_trip (int) – Down-projected edge embedding size (after Hadamard with RBF).

  • emb_size_bilinear (int) – Edge embedding size after the bilinear layer.

  • emb_size_rbf (int) – Radial basis embedding dimension.

  • emb_size_cbf (int) – Circular basis embedding dimension (one angle).

  • activation (str) – Activation function name.

  • scale_file (str | None) – Path to JSON scale-factor file, or None.

  • name (str) – Block name (used to look up scale factors).

__init__(emb_size_edge, emb_size_trip, emb_size_bilinear, emb_size_rbf, emb_size_cbf, activation, scale_file, name='TripletInteraction')[source]¶

Initialize TripletInteraction.

reset_parameters()[source]¶

Re-initialize all sub-layer weights.

Return type:

None

forward(m, rbf3, cbf3, Kidx3, id_swap, id3_expand_ba, id3_reduce_ca)[source]¶

Run the triplet interaction and return updated edge embeddings.

Parameters:

  • m (Tensor) – Edge embeddings, shape (nEdges, emb_size_edge).

  • rbf3 (Tensor) – Projected radial basis for triplets, shape (nEdges, emb_size_rbf).

  • cbf3 (tuple[Tensor, Tensor]) – Projected circular basis for triplets (tuple from EfficientInteractionDownProjection).

  • Kidx3 (Tensor) – Neighbor index for the triplet sparse dense matrix.

  • id_swap (Tensor) – Index mapping each edge to its reverse, shape (nEdges,).

  • id3_expand_ba (Tensor) – Triplet expand index b -> a, shape (nTriplets,).

  • id3_reduce_ca (Tensor) – Triplet reduce index c -> a, shape (nTriplets,).

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

Updated edge embeddings of shape (nEdges, emb_size_edge).

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.scaling module¶

Variance-preserving scale factors and JSON-loading utilities for GemNet calibration.

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.scaling.ScaleFactor(name=None)[source]¶

Bases: Module

Scalar variance-preserving factor.

The default value is 1.0 (identity / no scaling). A model loaded with scale_file=None therefore behaves as if no scaling were applied. Pre-fitted values are produced offline by scripts/gemnet_scale_fitting.py and persisted either via load_scales_json() (loading from the JSON the fitter writes) or automatically as part of the model’s state_dict.

The ref argument is accepted (and ignored) so call sites match the fairchem-style forward(x, ref=...) signature observed by the offline fitter via forward hooks.

Parameters:

name (str | None) – Optional human-readable label used as the JSON key when scales are fitted or loaded.

__init__(name=None)[source]¶

Initialize ScaleFactor.

scale_factor: Tensor¶
forward(x, ref=None)[source]¶

Apply the configured scale factor.

Parameters:

  • x (Tensor) – Input tensor to scale.

  • ref (Tensor | None) – Ignored reference tensor accepted for API compatibility.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

x multiplied by the stored scale factor.

class xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.scaling.ScalingFactor(scale_file=None, name=None, device=None)[source]¶

Bases: ScaleFactor

Legacy GemNet API shim: forward(x_ref, y) with the same semantics as ScaleFactor (scale y, observe x_ref as input).

The scale_file and device constructor arguments are accepted for backwards-compat with the pre-existing GemNet layer call sites but are ignored - scales are loaded by the model itself via load_scales_json() (or via state_dict round-trip).

Parameters:

  • scale_file (str | None) – Ignored legacy argument kept for constructor compatibility.

  • name (str | None) – Optional human-readable label used as the JSON key.

  • device (device | None) – Optional device used to place the scale parameter.

__init__(scale_file=None, name=None, device=None)[source]¶

Initialize ScalingFactor.

forward(x_ref, y)[source]¶

Apply the configured scale factor.

Parameters:

  • x_ref (Tensor) – Ignored reference tensor accepted for API compatibility.

  • y (Tensor) – Tensor to scale.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

y multiplied by the stored scale factor.

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.scaling.collect_scale_factors(model)[source]¶

Return all ScaleFactor submodules keyed by their dotted name.

Parameters:

model (Module) – Module tree to inspect.

Return type:

dict[str, ScaleFactor]

Returns:

Dictionary mapping dotted module paths to ScaleFactor instances.

xanesnet.models.gemnet.layers.scaling.load_scales_json(model, path, *, strict=False)[source]¶

Load fitted scale-factor values from a JSON file produced by scripts/gemnet_scale_fitting.py into model.

Missing keys are tolerated unless strict=True; the corresponding ScaleFactor submodules remain at 1.0 (identity).

Parameters:

  • model (Module) – Module whose ScaleFactor parameters to update.

  • path (str | Path) – Path to the JSON file produced by the scale fitter.

  • strict (bool) – If True, raise ValueError when any ScaleFactor key is absent from the file.

Return type:

int

Returns:

Number of scale factors successfully loaded.

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If path does not exist.

  • ValueError – If the file does not contain a JSON object, or if strict=True and a scale key is missing.
